target.name: Hailo
target.founder: Orr Danon, Hadar Zeitlin, Avi Baum

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $336M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Hailo is a Series D semiconductor company that has raised $336M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a universe of 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers in the chips and semiconductors sector, with particular focus on same-stage companies including SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, and DEEPX.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement to the $336M raised proxy, implying $504M. Peer-median anchoring across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies yields $2.7B, derived from a range spanning $529M (DEEPX, Series C) to $5.0B (SambaNova, Series D). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales cannot be applied; the public semiconductor universe trades at a median 25.3x revenue multiple, but Hailo's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $428M (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around two factors: whether Hailo's technology maturity and go-to-market traction align with higher-stage peers like SambaNova or lower-stage entrants like DEEPX, and the absence of revenue data that would anchor valuation to fundamental operating metrics. The peer-median point estimate ($2.7B) sits in the upper half of the range and reflects Series D positioning, but confidence remains constrained by limited comparability within frontier chip architectures.
